task.title: Loading the tipc module when the system boots

context.p: Before you can use the TIPC protocol, you must load the tipc kernel module. You can configure Red Hat Enterprise Linux to automatically load this kernel module automatically when the system boots.

step.cmd: Create the /etc/modules-load.d/tipc.conf file with the following content:
Restart the systemd-modules-load service to load the module without rebooting the system:

li.p: Use the following command to verify that RHEL loaded the tipc module:
If the command shows no entry for the tipc module, RHEL failed to load it.
